entry.title: Re: Celebrating 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano

My Name is Hafiz Adnan Ahmed, and I bring significant experience in the supply chain sector, particularly within the textile industry. After years of working in this domain, I have developed a deep understanding of the processes and challenges associated with supply chain management. However, I am now eager to transition my career towards SAP, as I believe it aligns well with my skills and interests. I am passionate about leveraging technology to optimize business processes, and SAP offers the perfect platform to achieve this. I would like to share that I fall under the following category

. People looking to switch their careers mid-life, and I am excited to embark on this new journey.

entry.title: Re: Celebrating 2 Years of the Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano and Digital Skills Initiative Team,

My name is Hammad Ali, and I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to the SAP team for their incredible efforts in launching the Digital Skills Initiative. This initiative has been immensely beneficial, providing access to high-quality resources and empowering learners like me to upskill effectively.

I am thrilled to have completed the "SAP Learning Journey Developing with SAP Integration Suite" program under the Digital Skills Initiative, and I am eager to leverage the opportunity to take a free certification attempt. This program is a shining testament to SAP's unwavering commitment to nurturing learning, growth, and innovation within the tech community.

Best Regards,
Hammad Ali.

entry.title: Re: AI in SAP Ariba Invoicing - Intelligent Invoice Conversion
entry.content:
With SAP Ariba 2411 (Nov 2024) Release, we have this new Feature for Intelligent Invoice Conversion:

Identify Buyer Using TaxID and PO number for Invoices Sent Through Email [NP-48723] 
This feature resolves the mandate of entering the ANID of the buyer in the subject line of the email. This feature now processes the invoice attached through mail correctly irrespective of whether the supplier enters the ANID or not. If ANID is not entered in the subject line of the email, the ANID of the buyer is identified from the attachment before conversion.

  •  Feature Preview
  •  5 minutes Demo

entry.title: Inspection point not created - doubt with key date of routing
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a problem with the generation of inspection point in relation to inspection type 03 when client creates manually a new routing, since with routing already created with massive migration there is not the problem.

So a Planner Production create a Routing and then a quality Responsible change the routing (with key date of routing) adding the inspection characteristic. First doubt: why if operation of routing validity is 20.11.24, the key date of characteristic is 21.11.24?

This key-date (21.11.24) is then viewable in the inspection lot 03 as key-date, instead usually the key date in inspection lot corresponds to date of release of production order. Why this difference? 

Besides in this case the production order has been release in 28.11.24, so both 20.11 and 21.11 should go well for the system to read inspection characteristic and creating then the inspection point.

Where can be the problem? Someone has some suggests?

Thank you in advance,

Lorenzo

entry.title: Re: Multiple email addresses for vendor communications using S4 HANA Output Management
entry.content:
This Functionality works when you maintain CC and BCC Email addresses in OPD bur not from the Business Partner.

Please suggest if my understanding is correct.

Regards

Siva

entry.title: Re: Usage-based subscription with minimum fixed consumpti...
entry.content:
On the first glance, it reads like you did the right steps.
As you said that no bill is created at all: did you check the rating monitor if there is an error listed for the usage rating?
And did you choose the correct unit of measure for the quantity field in the pricing field mapping for quantity1?

entry.title: Re: Cannot send audit log message to the backend message...
entry.content:
Hi,
Did you finally solve this problem? Do you remember how?
Thanks,
Simon

entry.title: Re: BAPI for transaction FB41 posting
entry.content: You can use a validation in OB28. Set up a validation to check if the tax code is missing for specific G/L accounts. If the tax code is empty, assign a default dummy tax code (e.g., XX) to bypass the error. This ensures compliance with account requirements while maintaining posting consistency.

entry.title: SAP Personas script loop Mass upade Equipment classification
entry.content:
Hi. I have a little bit tricky scenario.

I would like to mass update classification on equipment in transaction IE05 with this steps:

Multiple selection of equipment to be changed. Go to details.

It shows the first item of selected equipment. I have a classification on the left, which belongs to this equipment. No on the right I have custom fields, so I can select which classification I want to copy to other selected items.

Now, I have a button, that simulates "Save" of the equipment. By SAP standard it automatically goes to next selected item/equipment.

//Variable to be copied to all selected equipments
var vl = session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subPersonas_173150828914583/txtPersonas_173158981168825").text;
//button save
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/btn[11]").press()

//now I want to check if the equipment is actually there (so it stops at the end of selection)
if (session.idExists("wnd[0]/usr/subSUB_EQKO:SAPLITO0:0152/subSUB_0152A:SAPLITO0:1520/ctxtITOBATTR-EQUNR"))
{
    //just selecting the right tab
    session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTABSTRIP/tabpT\\03").select();
//now I want to enter the variable into selected equipment field
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/tabsTABSTRIP/tabpT\\03/ssubSUB_DATA:SAPLITO0:0109/subSUB_0109A:SAPLITO0:1093/subSUB_1090A:SAPLCTMS:4000/sub:SAPLCTMS:4000/ctxtRCTMS-MWERT[3,32]").text = vl;

//then I also enter variable in the same custom field, so it could read with next loop
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subPersonas_173150828914583/txtPersonas_173158981168825").text = vl;

//by now, I expet, that original variable vas transfered to next equipment and also put it self again for next equipment
//now I press the same script button, that originally started the script
    session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btnPersonas_17320111464853").press()}

The script successfully updates the second selected item, but not the rest

 

 

How could I do this loop, so it would go to all selected equipment and update the desired field?

entry.title: SAP Academic Community Conference Ibero-America 2024: What an Experience!
entry.content:
The SAP Academic Community Conference Ibero-America 2024  was an event to remember!

As you know, a key mission of SAP University Alliances is to build bridges between SAP, educational institutions, and the SAP ecosystem, and your engagement brings this vision to life! We were thrilled to have you with us for both our virtual sessions and at each of the six on-premises gatherings across SAP offices in Latin America.

Already feeling nostalgic for those #SAPACC moments? We’ve put together a quick recap to relive some of the highlights and celebrate the insights, discussions, and connections that made this event memorable...

 

#SAPACC recap – November 4th

Virtual Webinar

Our #SAPACC week kicked off with an inspiring webinar introducing SAP Next-Gen, showcasing our offerings to the academic community, and featuring several special sessions with SAP leaders on various key segments.

With over 190 participants, 8 hours of content, and 7 sessions, the event provided a unique opportunity to explore SAP’s of and the diverse opportunities for academic collaboration.

Participants explored key topics such as Cloud, Artificial Intelligence (AI), Sustainability, and Diversity & Inclusion (D&I), while also connecting with other members of our community. This session highlighted the valuable resources within SAP University Alliances that support your journey both in and beyond the classroom!

Looking for a recap of any sessions? You can access recordings and slides here: 

  • Opening: Welcome to the SAP Next-Gen World with Dr. Karina Edmonds  
  • SAP University Alliances with Maria Calzada & Gloria Sandi 
  • Academic Board Ibero-America with Jesus Aguillar 
  • Cloud at SAP with Alexandre Pereira & special guest
  • Sustainability at SAP with Renata Amorim 
  • AI at SAP with Matheus Souza 
  • Diversity and Inclusion at SAP with Ezequiel Massa 
  • Slides

 

#SAPACC recap – November 8th

Bogotá, Colombia

Our Bogotá event brought together 25 participants, who engaged in seven insightful sessions led by SAP experts and special panelists from Universidad de Los Andes and ERP4students.

Buenos Aires, Argentina

In Buenos Aires, 13 attendees participated in eight sessions. Contributions came from SAP experts, alongside panel discussions featuring leading institutions such as UNTREF, UADE, ERPsimLab, and a special appearance by Tecnológico de Monterrey.

Lima, Peru

Our event in Lima welcomed 24 participants who took part in four engaging sessions led by SAP experts and complemented by panels with insights from special guests from Universidad Peruana de Ciencias Aplicadas and Universidad de Lima.

Monterrey, Mexico

The Monterrey event brought together 24 attendees with an agenda featuring seven sessions presented by SAP experts and special panelists from Tecnológico de Monterrey offering valuable insights.

Santiago, Chile

In Santiago, 20 participants joined us for five sessions, featuring perspectives from our guest panelists from Universidad de las Américas and Universidad Bernardo O'Higgins, along with other special sessions led by SAP experts.

São Leopoldo, Brazil

Our largest event, in São Leopoldo, gathered 66 participants for ten inspiring sessions with SAP experts and contributions from professors from leading institutions such as FESP, FSA, Mackenzie, UCS, and UFRGS.

 

Thank you once again to everyone who attended our events, especially those who submitted panels and contributed their incredible insights and knowledge to the SAP Academic Community. It was a true pleasure to engage with each of you and learn about the amazing work being done, as well as the exciting plans for the future!

Share your favorite moments or key takeaways from the event in the comments below. We look forward to continuing to work together and strengthen our collaboration moving forward 💙

entry.title: Re: Invoicing in SAP Ariba
entry.content:
With SAP Ariba 2411 (Nov 2024) Release, we have these 5 new Features for Invoices:

  1. Invoice Error Message Categorization Using Generative AI (NP-38941)
    This feature automatically converts verbose rejection text into concise, easy-to-understand and actionable rejection categories. As a supplier, you can now use GenAI capability inside the invoice document to transform detailed rejection reasons into rejection categories for further analysis. Supplier needs to activate the Gen AI preference for Invoice & follow few steps to get the feature working.
    Feature Preview
    6 minutes Demo

  2. Communication of Summarized Rejection Categories [NP-38942]
    With this feature, you can send periodic (monthly) invoice rejection summary to supplier via automated email. A summary rejection email with top 15 external rejections and top 15 business network rejections is sent for every transaction relationship to the supplier every month with the details on rejection. Supplier needs to switch on network notification. Also, feature NP-38941 (Invoice Error Message Categorization Using Generative AI) is a prerequisite.
    Feature Preview
    6 minutes Demo

  3. E-Invoicing in Romania for Inbound Invoices [NG-6481]
    SAP Business Network now supports electronic tax invoicing in Romania for inbound invoices (invoices created by suppliers in a system outside SAP Business Network).
    Feature Preview
  4. Invoices Analytics for Suppliers [BCS3-2488]
    With this feature, SAP Business Network offers data analytics on your invoices to help you better understand your customers and business. The information is presented in the Invoices Overview view of the new Analytics dashboard in the SAP Business Network user interface for suppliers.
    Note:
    This feature is not available to all customers on the date of the 2411 Release for Business Network. It is planned to be rolled out in phases, with all customers enabled by December 31st. It needs configuration & you must have a SAP Business Network Enterprise account at Platinum subscription level for Suppliers.
    Feature Preview
    4 minutes Demo
    12 minutes Demo

  5. Intelligent Invoice Conversion - Identify Buyer Using TaxID and PO number for Invoices Sent Through Email [NP-48723]
    This feature resolves the mandate of entering the ANID of the buyer in the subject line of the email. This feature now processes the invoice attached through mail correctly irrespective of whether the supplier enters the ANID or not. If ANID is not entered in the subject line of the email, the ANID of the buyer is identified from the attachment before conversion.
    Feature Preview
    5 minutes Demo

entry.title: Re: Optimizing Tactical Sourcing: Maximizing Benefits from this Guided Buying Channel
entry.content:
Dear @Moutrisha_Saha, 

By default, all supplier contacts are invited to participate in the RFQs submitted from Guided Buying.

When SAP Ariba Sourcing license is available, in certain scenarios it is possible to edit this list of contacts per RFQ.

And, since November release, if you also have SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance solution, it's possible to select which are supplier primary contact and bid/proposal manager and send the RFQ only to them.

Supplier contacts selection per Guided Buying RFQ scenario
In Self Service scenario (the only one available without having an SAP Ariba Sourcing license) there is no option to modify the supplier contacts invited to the RFQ.

While in other RFQ scenarios, like Low Touch, the RFQ content could be reviewed and edited in SAP Ariba Sourcing before the RFQ was published and sent to suppliers. This includes the option to edit the suppliers contacts invited to the RFQ

New "Additional Supplier Contact Information on Multi-Line Request for Quotes" in 2411 release
Now, with 2411 release, the new feature called GB-28422: Additional Supplier Contact Information on Multi-Line Request for Quotes allows Guided Buying administrators to decide whether the system, by default, will all supplier contacts to each RFQ or just the supplier's primary contact and the bid/proposal manager.

This feature is enabled with new ENABLE_SUPPLIER_CONTACT_SELECTION_IN_RFQ parameter and requires.
Prerequisites are PARAM_ENABLE_ENHANCED_RFQ_INTEGRATION and PARAM_ENABLE_EDIT_RFQ parameters that are explained in my blog above, in order to enable new GB RFQ Form and API communication that is required for all GB RFQ enhancements released since end of 2020.

Supplier's contact types are maintained in SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance solution (license required).

In summary:

  • By default all supplier contacts are invited
  • With new 2411 feature and SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance solution it's possible to define a limited list of supplier's contacts to be invited
  • With SAP Ariba Sourcing solution it's possible to edit supplier contacts that are invited by default from Guided Buying, in Low Touch and High Touch scenarios.

Kind regards,

Jorge

entry.title: Clean Core on-prem, pratical meaning
entry.content:
Hi all,

we're running S/4 HANA 2023 FP1 on-prem. We came from ECC system by brownfield approach. This means, we simply migrated our 20+yrs development Z-objects over.

Now I want to clean up this mess and become more clean core compliant. To be honest, we can't be even more far away from clean core compliant. Also there are currently no plans to move to any cloud scenario. But at least I want to be prepared.

I read through lots of blogs, Devtoberfest and TechEd and now have a good feeling of what clean core means. Now I try to analyze our clean core index struggling with what it means.

What is clear to me is:
- get rid of user exists (~130)
- get rid of modifications (~850)
- get rid of SAPScript (~50)
- get rid of implicit enhancements.

What about all the other objects? 
- DDIC domains, data elements tables
- BTE events
- function modules (several hundret)
- Z-report and includes (~2000)
- SQ01/SQ02
- copy rules (SD), which are some kind of modification
- alreads implemented BAdI

We will not become 100% clean core compliant but I want to get as close as possible. Especially all these Z-reports drives me crazy. What does clean core means for these? Do I need to replace all of them by custom API and new UI to become compliant? We still use only few Fiori Apps and most users and processes stick to SAP GUI.

Do I need to supersed each funciton module with an API?
In SD there are many modifications like copy rules or condition rules. How to replace these?

How to handle all BAdI implementations already done? They're not visible in "Custom Code and Logic" App and I don't think it makes sense to re-implement them there?

Which other objects do I need to take into account? As a first indicator, I try to change ABAP language and found that several objects (like reports or FM) doesn't support this. Is this a valid approach?

Greets from the beginning of a long journey
BR Patrick

entry.title: Templates on Disclosure Management
entry.content:
In this blog, we’ll take a step-by-step approach to demystify templates in Disclosure Management. From creation to configuration and application, you’ll learn practical tips and best practices to build and use templates that streamline your reporting workflows.

Let’s get started on creating templates that work smarter, not harder!

 

The Report Template defines the overall structure of the Report, including headers, footers, page numbering, text and sections.

  • This ensures that all Reports generated within SAP DM follow a consistent format.
  • It controls the placement of content such as tables, charts and textual data, ensuring they are positioned appropriately throughout the Report.

While Templates provide a standardized base, users can still make custom adjustments to fit specific reporting needs, such as adding custom footnotes, sections or disclaimers.

DM custom styles are recommended to use due to possible issues with file opening by someone with different default styles, for stable content refresh and ad-hoc previewing - please check note 2448528: https://me.sap.com/notes/2448528 for word formatting, styles and templates⁠.

 

Create a Template

When you want to create a Template for the Report, click on the full report and go to "Templates" tab.

In this tab, there will be three options:

  1. Available Templates
  2. Upload Templates
  3. Delete Templates

1. Available Templates

In this option you can check on the available Templates already existing and if needed you can Edit it by selecting the Template you want to edit and clicking on "Edit" button.

2. Upload Templates

In this option you can either upload an already existing Template from your files or build a new Template and upload it by clicking on "Choose File" > "Open" > "Add Template".

Here you can upload different Templates such as cover page, auditor's report, chapters, etc.

Word Template and styles logic in SAP DM

Master Template – defines report styles, headers, footers, etc.

Chapter N.doc – for each chapter you define Chapter configuration. Chapter has content, it might not have header and footer which can be inherited from the Master Template for the Word Report.

In case you use a different font in a chapter and you don’t want to change it (for example you used Calibri but you wanted to use Arial), you can define that the Word Report will be getting the style from the Master Template (define the style there). This will allow you not to go to each chapter and change the style there – just define it in Master Template and use it for Word Report.

In case one chapter has its own style, for example a different header and footer and you want to use it in the Word Report, mention it in the Chapter configuration.

As previously mentioned, it is recommended to create DM styles that are not based on any of the standard Word styles. In Word, select the Home tab and open styles and click on "Add style". Fill the properties and formatting accordingly to the style wanted. To better identify the styles created, it is recommended to give the style a name starting by "DM_stylename", for example "DM_BodyText".

Once the Template is finished, please notice that when saving the Template, you have to save it as "Word Template" as you can see in the picture below:

3. Delete Templates

In case you want to delete a Template, select the Template you want to delete and click on "delete" as you can see below:

 

Configuration on chapter level

You will then have to choose configuration options for each chapter. Set these configurations by clicking in each chapter and then click on the "Configuration" tab.

The entire documents gets generated by combining chapters.

This is done based on these parameters on chapter level.

Before creating the final Report, you should set configuration for all the chapters.

Open the top Report level, check on the Template you want to use - you must mention one of the Templates from this top Report level Template for each chapter in "Configuration" tab. 

The most common template should be called "Default.docx" because that's the default input in chapter configuration, when chapter is just created.

In this case, the only available Template is "Default.dotx"

When setting the configuration for Template name mention one of the Report Template's that you have available as you can see in the pictures below:

Once this is done, you will be able to create the Report with the corresponding Template to each chapter.

 

Applying Template

In case you have done any changes in Template that you want to apply to the rest of the chapters, there is a quick way to apply those changes.

Go to Report level, click on "lock" to lock Report and chapters > click on "Rollout templates" and all chapters will be updated according to the Report's Template > click on "Unlock" and the changes will be applied.

entry.title: How to Use and Configure the Transport Center Tool for SuccessFactors: Tips and Tricks
entry.content:
SuccessFactors, SAP's cloud-based Human Capital Management (HCM) suite, offers a variety of tools to streamline HR processes. One key tool within SuccessFactors is the Transport Center Tool, a critical component for managing and migrating configurations across different SuccessFactors environments. Whether you're moving configurations from a development to a test environment, or transferring them to production, mastering the Transport Center is essential for ensuring that your changes are implemented seamlessly.

In this blog post, we'll explore how to use and configure the Transport Center tool for SuccessFactors and provide you with some helpful tips and tricks to make the process more efficient.

What is the Transport Center Tool?

The Transport Center Tool in SuccessFactors is a central hub that facilitates the migration of configurations and customizations across different environments (such as Development, Testing, and Production) within your SuccessFactors landscape. It helps manage changes made to your SuccessFactors instance and ensures that those changes can be transferred safely and efficiently between environments.

Some of the common use cases for the Transport Center include:

  • Moving configuration changes from one environment to another.
  • Transporting metadata, such as employee data models or custom objects.
  • Managing system updates.

The CTC helps maintain consistency and control over your SuccessFactors configuration by offering a structured way to handle these changes.

How to Access the Transport Center Tool in SuccessFactors

Before diving into the configuration of the Transport Center, you first need to access the tool. Here’s how to do that:

  1. Login to SuccessFactors: Use your administrator credentials to log into the SuccessFactors system.
  2. Navigate to the Admin Center: Once logged in, go to the Admin Center by clicking on the "Admin Center" tab in the top menu.
  3. Search for the Transport Center: In the Admin Center, use the search bar to look for the "Transport Center" tool. Click on the search result to open the Transport Center interface.

If you have the necessary permissions, you'll be able to configure and manage the transport jobs from here.

Key Features of the Transport Center Tool

Before configuring the Transport Center, it’s helpful to understand its key features:

  • Transport Jobs: These are the individual tasks that represent a configuration change or set of changes that need to be moved from one environment to another.
  • Import and Export: You can export configuration changes from one environment and import them into another.
  • Package Management: Transport jobs can be grouped into packages for easier management and tracking.
  • Version Control: Transport jobs maintain versioning, making it easy to track changes and revert to previous configurations when necessary.

Step-by-Step Guide to Using the Transport Center Tool

Step 1: Create a Transport Job

Creating a transport job is the first step in using the Transport Center tool. Here's how to create one:

  1. In the Transport Center interface, click on Create Transport Job.
  2. Give your transport job a meaningful name (for example, "Employee Central Config Changes").
  3. Select the Source System (the environment you're exporting from) and the Target System (the environment you're importing to).
  4. Choose the Configuration Type. This could be anything from Employee Central, Recruiting, or Performance Management depending on what you're migrating.
  5. Once the job is created, you’ll need to add the objects or configurations that you want to transport. Click Add Object and select the items you want to include in the job.

Step 2: Export the Transport Job

Once you've created the transport job and added the necessary configurations, it’s time to export it.

  1. In the Transport Center interface, locate the transport job you just created.
  2. Click on Export. This action will prepare the job for migration from the source system to the target system.
  3. Depending on the size of the transport job and the number of configurations, the export process may take a few minutes.

Step 3: Import the Transport Job

After exporting, you’ll need to import the transport job into the target system.

  1. In the Transport Center, switch to the Target System.
  2. Locate the exported transport job under Available Jobs.
  3. Click on Import to begin the transfer process. The system will check the job and ensure all configurations are compatible before proceeding.
  4. Once the import is successful, verify that the configurations are correctly applied in the target system.

Step 4: Test the Transport Job

It’s crucial to test that everything is working as expected after the transport job has been completed. This is especially important in environments like testing or production.

  1. Log into the target environment (for example, the test or production environment).
  2. Check that the configurations, metadata, or custom objects have been correctly applied.
  3. Conduct thorough testing to ensure that everything is functioning as expected. If issues arise, you may need to revise the transport job or troubleshoot any compatibility problems.

Best Practices for Using the Transport Center Tool

While the Transport Center tool is relatively straightforward, here are some best practices to keep in mind for a smooth experience:

1. Use Descriptive Names for Transport Jobs

Give your transport jobs meaningful names that will help you and your team quickly identify what each job does. A clear naming convention (such as "EC_Config_Changes_Dev_to_Test") helps avoid confusion, especially when managing multiple transport jobs. Also try to differentiate fix and enhancements bundles to help the identification of possible bundle errors.

2. Test in a Non-Production Environment

Always test your transport jobs in a non-production environment (such as a sandbox or testing environment) before moving them to production. This helps identify any issues early on and ensures that the configuration works as expected without disrupting live operations.

3. Use Transport Packages

For larger projects, group related transport jobs into packages. This allows you to manage related configurations together and reduces the complexity of transporting them individually. Packages can be version-controlled, making rollback easier if something goes wrong.

4. Monitor Transport Job Status

Keep an eye on the status of your transport jobs to ensure everything is running smoothly. Use the monitoring tools in the CTC to track job progress and quickly identify any issues or errors.

5. Follow Version Control Best Practices

It’s important to keep track of the versions of the configurations you’re transporting. Always document changes and maintain version control to ensure you can easily roll back to a previous version if needed.

6. Caution with release cycles

During the gap between preview and production you may get some errors related to software versions differences, so please plan ahead your transportations

7. Search is tricky

During the search, if you change de filter criteria and click on go, you will not get any info, instead of it, you need to click on the left inside configuration area.

8. Isn't the tool to copy all configurations

You have the limitation on 300 results per search and 50 items per bundle, so if you plan to move a lot of configuration, you will need to break it in several bundles.

9. Attention on RBP copy

If you try to copy groups with filters that point out to data that doesn't exist you will get an error, so be sure you have all the relevant data before copy RBP

 

Troubleshooting Tips

Even with careful planning, issues may arise. Here are some troubleshooting tips for common issues you might encounter:

  • Transport Job Fails: Check the error message and verify that the configuration in the source and target environments is compatible. Sometimes, a mismatch in versions or missing dependencies can cause a transport job to fail.
  • Configuration Not Appearing: If the configuration doesn’t appear in the target environment after the import, verify the transport job’s log for errors and confirm that the import process was completed successfully.
  • Permissions Issues: Ensure you have the appropriate permissions to access and manage the Transport Center. Lack of permissions can prevent you from creating, exporting, or importing transport jobs.

Conclusion

The CTC in SuccessFactors is an essential utility for moving configurations across environments. Whether you're updating a single object or migrating an entire set of configurations, mastering the Transport Center helps ensure that changes are made safely and consistently. By following best practices, naming conventions, and thorough testing, you can avoid common pitfalls and streamline your SuccessFactors configuration management process.

Now that you have a better understanding of how to use and configure the Transport Center tool, you can implement these tips and tricks to maximize its effectiveness and ensure successful migrations every time. Happy transporting!

entry.title: Consumo de FUE no SAP S/4HANA Cloud: uma visão geral em português
entry.content:
Há um mês atrás, li um post no LinkedIn , da Melorine Parsy, que me deixou, no mínimo, curiosa! O post iniciava assim:

 

  • 73% dos clientes de ECC entendem parcialmente ou não entendem a estrutura de licenciamento da SAP.

 

Ela estava falando especificamente do licenciamento de SAP S/4HANA, para pensar e planejar um projeto de conversão ou migração do ECC ao SAP S/4HANA.

 

Independente da porcentagem ser esta ou não, fiquei intrigada e pensando como poderia minimizar este gap de conhecimento. E seja você cliente ou parceiro, deixo aqui um vídeo para que você possa iniciar seu conhecimento e saber onde mais buscar informações.

 

Mas antes do vídeo, já digo que a venda atual de RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition é feita com base em FUE (Full Use Equivalent), que categoriza o tipo de uso que o usuário terá no sistema: Advanced, Core, Self-service ou Developer. E isso é baseado nas autorizações/permissões que os usuários têm.

Existem dois artigos bem explicativos que falam de FUE, escritos por @pascalrenet, que sugiro a leitura:

Parte 1: Did you say FUE? A definition.

Parte 2: Thoughts on how to plan, monitor and control your FUE usage

 

Poxa, mas não sou vendedor, porque tenho que saber isso?

 

FUE se traduz em autorizações que o usuário tem para acessar o sistema SAP S/4HANA.

Lembra da transação SU01 no SAP ECC para criar e atribuir permissões a um usuário? Como você, consultor, fará essa atribuição no SAP S/4HANA? E as autorizações dadas aos usuários estão de acordo com os a quantidade de licenças (FUE) compradas pelo cliente? Aliás, você fez handover com o time de vendas para eles lhe contarem sobre o que foi vendido, certo?

 

Ou então você é um Customer Success Manager/Partner e está acompanhando e monitorando o uso e consumo de FUE’s do seu cliente. Você sabe explicar a métrica ao seu cliente? Você consegue interagir com o time de projeto ou consultoria para guiar qual report pode ser executado no SAP S/4HANA para ter detalhes de usuários vs. FUE?

 

 

No vídeo, de apenas 10 minutos, meu intuito é lhe mostrar rapidamente:

✔️O que está incluso na licença de RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition?

✔️Como as autorizações dos usuários do S/4HANA refletem no consumo de FUE?

✔️Como monitorar uso e consumo de FUE do ambiente S/4HANA Cloud?

 

Todos os sites apresentados estão na descrição do vídeo, para você navegar, aprender e disseminar o conhecimento.

entry.title: Where can I learn how to configure key record to report elements in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring the Record-to-Report Process in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey empowers business users and consultants to configure key Record-to-Report elements that are essential for effective month-end close processes. This knowledge is particularly beneficial when defining and designing business processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Learning objectives
Upon completing this learning journey, you will be able to configure advanced valuation of foreign currency and reclassification of payables/receivables, execute and review validation rule runs, and utilize these processes effectively during month-end close to ensure accuracy and compliance.

Prerequisites

  • Designing the Record-to-Report Process in SAP S/4HANA

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: synchronization between employee and business partner...
entry.content:
Hello,

When an employee has two or more personnel numbers, the first BP is always created with role BUP003 (employee) for the first personnel number.

For creating an additional BP for the second personnel number, the second personnel number must have a bank account maintained in infotype 9 and maintain a reference BP (with vendor) for the company code of the second personnel in view PTRV_VENDOR_SYNC.

There are some fields which are mandatory at vendor side, such as as the reconciliation account, that do not exist in HR, so the value of this fields is copied from the reference BP from view PTRV_VENDOR_SYNC.

Hello,

You can add the following lines to your code:

    // Get the body of the message as a String
    def body = message.getBody(String)

    // the below code solves the non-breaking space issue
    String nbspHTML = ' '
    String nbspXML = ' '
    body = ((String)body).replaceAll(nbspHTML, nbspXML)

BR,

Andrzej

entry.title: SAP Business Client & SAP GUI: Not launching GUI for Windows
entry.content:
Dear experts,

we are trying to experiment with the hybrid Fiori/GUI expierence, described in this Devtobertfest session: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ihxl85uXrjc

We have installed the newest versions of NWBC and GUI on a local machine and created a connection with type "Fiori launchpad", the GUI fallback is set to "SAP GUI for Windows". FLP can be opened and operated without problems.

However, when we try to launch a classical transaction like VA02, NWBC does not launch an embedded version of SAP GUI for Windows but instead tries to launch SAP GUI for HTML (which fails with an error saying "This location is not explicitly marked as safe in Internet Explorer zone model").

Are we missing a setting here? The way I understood the session was that SAP NWBC should not launch the webgui service but an embedded version of SAP GUI for Windows? Otherwise there would be no point in using NWBC over the browser based FLP.

entry.title: How to configure Work Center for complex situation of determining machine capacity
entry.content:
Hi Product Experts,

Please guide on the below unqiue situation.  

We have complex situation of configuring work centre for capacity planning due to combination of machine, tool width, tool characterises.

  • There are several machines and multiple tools which can run on each machine either specifically or alternatively. Some tools are very specific to the machine, while other tools can be adopted on other machines in case of capacity overload.
  • Tool characteristics consists of one or more surfaces and density of output it can produce.
  • The machine capacity/output is determined by the tool width and tool characteristics as it produces unique output.

Work centre, tool width and surface combinations and variations

There is 5 hours set up time for every change in Tool from A to B and so on.

There is 30 minutes set up time for change in tool characteristics i.e. surface and density.

Based on above information below is situation for illustration:

On Machine 100 three different tools A, B, C can be installed, and each of the tool has different width and tool characteristics in which output can be produced.

The machine can run with one of the tools (A/B/C) and surface (SW/LV) at a time, the available capacity is cumulation of the output from the machine with different tool settings (A+B+C) and surface.

All tools are not freely interchangeable between machine 100, 200 and 300.  Some Tools have no alternate machines on which they can run, they are tied with the machine. Like Tool B, C, D, F and G are specific to machine.  While Tool A can be installed on Machine 100 and 200, but on Machine 200 tool D get priority as it doesn’t have alternate machine on which it can run. Likewise, Tool E can also be installed on Machine 200 and 300, but on machine 300 tools F and G gets priority as they don’t have alternate machine on which they can run.

Problem statement

  • How to create work centre which can handle combination of machine and tool with the objective to keep tool setup time and characteristics change over time to minimum?
  • Production controller must be able to plan the orders in sequence without need to change tool frequently as this would reduce the overall availability of machine for production.
  • Capacity evaluation of the work centre in combination of machine and tool must be at once in single view.

Shabbir Kachwala

entry.title: SAP-Ariba-Help-Center – Lieferanten erhalten Support
entry.content:
Thank you to my my colleague Rebecca Dolan who help in translating this blog.

SAP-Ariba-Lieferanten können Support erhalten, indem sie einen Rückruf von der SAP-Ariba-Supportseite anfordern.

So gehen Sie vor:

Gehen Sie auf https://support.ariba.com/help.

Dies führt Sie zu dem folgenden Screen (Sie müssen sich keine Sorgen machen, wenn Sie sich nicht anmelden können).

Die Sprache kann über das Dropdown-Symbol in der oberen rechten Ecke geändert werden.

Wählen Sie eine der folgenden Optionen aus.

Sie können eine Beschreibung Ihrer Anfrage eingeben. Wählen Sie Suche, um relevante Artikel zu erhalten, die bei Ihrer Frage helfen könnten.

Wenn die Artikel Ihre Anfrage nicht beantworten, können Sie mit einem SAP-Support-Mitarbeiter sprechen, indem Sie auf

Kontakt in der linken oberen Ecke klicken.

Wählen Sie dann Anmeldungshilfe, wie im Screenshot gezeigt

Hier sehen Sie dann weitere Optionen angezeigt. Wählen Sie bitte Ich habe ein anderes Problem, um fortzufahren.

Rechts unten könne Sie nun auf Fall erstellen klicken.

Ganz oben haben Sie die Möglichkeit die Sprache auszuwählen, in der Sie kontaktiert werden möchten.

Füllen Sie dann alle erforderlich Felder (mit roten Sternchen) im Formular aus, und gehen Sie sicher, dass Sie am Ende des Formulars das Häkchen bei Ich stimme zu setzten.

Sie müssen Details zu Ihrem Problem sowie Ihre Kontaktdaten eingeben. Bitte geben Sie auch Ihre ANID-Nummer an. Sie finden Ihre SAP-Ariba-Network-ID (ANID), sobald Sie auf Ihrem SAP-Ariba-Konto angemeldet sind. Die ANID-Nummer Ihres Kontos wird angezeigt, wenn Sie oben rechts in der Anwendung auf Ihre Initialen > Einstellungen klicken.

Nachdem Sie Ihre Details eingegeben haben, können Sie unten rechts auf Ein letzter Schritt klicken.

Sie sehen nun ein Telefonsymbol und die geschätzte Wartezeit. Sie können auswählen, ob der Anruf aufgezeichnet werden soll.

Der letzte Schritt zum Anfordern eines Rückrufs vom SAP-Ariba-Supplier-Support-Team besteht darin, Ihren Fall einzureichen, indem Sie auf OK klicken.

SAP-Ariba-Käufer

Für den Käufersupport gehen Sie bitte auf SAP for Me.

Wenn Sie Probleme beim Zugriff auf SAP Ariba haben, wenden Sie sich an die Ihnen zugewiesene Support-Kontaktperson (Designated Support Contact).

Die Ihnen zugewiesene Support-Kontaktperson finden Sie unter Administration & Mehr -> Kontakte.

3121688 - S-User-ID-Berechtigungen für Zugriff auf zugewiesene Support-Kontaktperson oder einfachen Zugriff - SAP Ariba Connect

Wenn Sie weitere Unterstützung vom SAP-Ariba-Support benötigen, können Sie einen Fall in SAP for Me Portal anlegen. Dazu benötigen Sie eine S-User-ID, die die benötigten Berechtigungen hat um Fälle anzulegen.

Informationen zum Anlegen eines Falls finden Sie unter 1296527 - Anlegen eines support-Falls für SAP Support - SAP for Me.

Danke fürs Lesen!

Wir hoffen, dass Sie diesen Artikel hilfreich fanden. Sie können sich jederzeit gerne an das Customer Interaction Center wenden, wenn Sie Hilfe mit den oben aufgeführten Schritten benötigen.

Wir würden gerne von Ihnen hören, lassen Sie uns wissen, was Sie denken?

Lesen Sie hier mehr Blogbeiträge vom Customer Interaction Center!

Weitere verwandte Themen

 SAP Ariba Network für Lieferanten: Häufig gestellte Frage

Wie wende ich mich als Lieferant an den SAP-Business-Network-Kundensupport?

Hier können Sie eine Demo anfordern oder sich an das SAP-Ariba-Sales-Team wenden.

entry.title: Where can I learn how to prepare and run a Business AI Explore Workshop?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Applying a Human-Centered Approach to Identify and Define Business AI Use Cases learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey explains in detail how to run two interactive sessions aimed at exploring and defining AI use cases based on business and user needs. 
Challenge: Spot the Differences and Win!
Throughout the lessons of this training, our crafty speakers have made some sneaky changes in the set. Can you catch them?
Your Mission: Watch closely all videos and note differences in the set between videos from different lessons (e.g. different plant between lesson 1 and 3). Note as many as possible and send your list to apphaus@sap.com.
What's in it for you?
The first 10 participants to send 20 differences will receive a printed set of the Opportunity, Limitation, and Ideation cards - perfect for your Business AI Explore and Design workshops!

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Prepare and run a Business AI Explore Workshop with strategic decision makers to ideate and identify AI use cases with business value.
  • Prepare and run a Business AI Design workshop with business and IT experts to understand user requirements for a use case idea or existing process, ideate valuable AI capabilities to improve it, and define a future user experience enriched with AI.

 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Can we discuss SAP ABAP Platform 2022 SP01, Developer Edition on Cloud Appliance Library (CAL)
entry.content:
Hi 

I'm generally interested in ABAP testdrives. I don't use them for much, but for more than 20 years I have seen it as a challenge to make the newest version run.
I have currently ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 SP01 running on Docker and this time I am actually using the testdrive to evaluate if it is also time for me to switch from SE38 to Eclipse 🤔

I can see that this version (Developer Trial 2022 SP01) also exist in a CAL / cloud version, but i'm not sure it is actually the same product? When reading the getting started guide, I get the impression that it is actually the same product - just running in cloud instead of on-premise(docker), but when I read the recommended VM sizes I don't understand anything.

The recommended size for the cloud version is 32 cores and 256 GB memory, but I run the Docker version on a labtop with 10 cores in total and out of 32 GB ram, only 20 GB is assigned to Docker . 

The cost of a Cloud VM with 32 cores and 256 GB is astronomic (from a freelance ABAP'ers point of view) and I find it hard to believe that anyone use this option?

  1. Why is the the required memory for the cloud version more than 10 times higher than the Docker version?
  2. Did I completely misunderstand everything?

@julieplummer20 

Regards Thomas 

PS. I didn't really find a suitable "SAP Managed Tag" for this question, so I hope that it hits a suitable place in the Community.

entry.title: Re: Log into new Client with SAP*
entry.content:
Hello Victor,

you need to delete SAP* user from SU01 in the respective client you want to log in with SAP* Kerneluser. There is a difference between SAP* User in SU01 and SAP* User in Kernel. 

Furthermore login/no_automatic_user_sapstar is client independent it is valid for all clients, also you need to restart your system after changing this parameter. SCC4 is also client independent.

so go on like this

- delete SAP* user from SU01 in client 101

- set login/no_automatic_user_sapstar to 0 

- restart system

after that you should be able to login into client 101 with Kerneluser SAP* and pass

password of SAP* is release dependent i guess, but pass should do the trick

 

best regards

entry.title: Insights Needed: Managing Complex Relationships with Secondary Hierarchy in SAP SuccessFactors Compe
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m currently working on the implementation of SAP SuccessFactors Compensation and would like to gather your insights and experiences regarding the management of complex relationships using the secondary hierarchy feature.

In our scenario, we need to account for additional relationships in the compensation process, including:

Distinct final approvers based on employee `groups`.

Specific tracking roles for certain types of employees.

An extended hierarchy chain involving employees and managers across different legal entities.

I’m particularly looking to understand how the secondary hierarchy in Compensation can be configured to:

Manage these complex relationships without disrupting the standard hierarchy.

Enable flexibility in workflows for salary and bonus reviews.

Ensure the system remains performant and maintainable, even with a large user base.

If any of you have faced similar challenges or implemented effective solutions, I would love to hear your thoughts! Any advice on best practices or key points to keep in mind would also be greatly appreciated.

To provide some context, here are the key links for remuneration roles and their descriptions in our setup:

Valideur_Final_Rémunération_Classes_Groupe_A: Final approver for Group A or Class.

Valideur_Final_Rémunération_Groupes_B_C: Final approver for Groups B or C.

Manager_Rémunération_Classes_Groupe_A: Submitter for Group A or Class.

Manager_Rémunération_Groupes_B_C: Submitter for Groups B or C.

RH_Rémunération_Classes_Groupe_A: HR monitor for Group A or Class.

RH_Rémunération_Groupes_B_C: HR monitor for Groups B or C.

Thank you in advance for your insights and advice!

Regards,
